text.title: task-spec-kit-three-step Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for task-spec-kit-three-step. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: The agent MUST run `/speckit.specify` before `/speckit.plan`, and `/speckit.plan` before `/speckit.tasks`. The agent MUST NOT edit any source file until `tasks.md` exists, lists the file in scope, and is checked into the feature branch. Any unresolved `[NEEDS CLARIFICATION]` marker in `spec.md` blocks plan generation.
rule.rationale: The spec is the only artifact that survives model swaps and conversation truncation; the plan is the only artifact that records architectural rationale for review; tasks.md is the only artifact that decomposes the work into parallelizable, AC-anchored units the coding agent can attempt independently. Reordering or skipping breaks the audit chain.

rule.statement: plan.md MUST contain a "Constitution Gates" section enumerating each principle with one of three values: `pass` (with one-sentence evidence), `waived` (with reviewer name and link to the waiver issue), or `blocked` (which halts the pipeline). The agent MUST NOT generate tasks.md while any gate is `blocked`. Waivers are only valid if signed by a CODEOWNER and reference an issue ID.
rule.rationale: Without recorded evidence, the gate degenerates into a comment the LLM ignores. By forcing a value plus citation per principle, the plan becomes auditable and the cost of skipping a principle (a CODEOWNER review) is now non-zero — which is what makes the gate behavioural.
